policy_name: Coagulation Testing QC and Specimen Collection Policy
purpose: This policy describes specimen collection, handling, and QC for coagulation testing (PT/INR, aPTT, fibrinogen, D-dimer, thrombin time, factor assays). Coagulation specimen integrity is uniquely sensitive to collection technique and time-to-testing; required by 42 CFR 493.1232 (specimen integrity), 42 CFR 493.1242 (handling), and 42 CFR 493.1256 (controls).

cfr_text_blocks.label: Standard: Control procedures (each run)
cfr_text_blocks.verbatim: Perform control material testing as specified in this paragraph (d) before resuming patient testing when a complete change of reagents is introduced; major preventive maintenance is performed; or any critical part that may influence test performance is replaced.

scope: All <<LAB_NAME>> personnel collecting or testing coagulation specimens.
policy_statements: Coagulation specimens are collected in sodium citrate tubes (typically blue-top, 3.2% sodium citrate) with attention to the 9:1 blood-to-citrate ratio; underfilled or overfilled tubes are rejected.
Time from collection to centrifugation and to testing is documented and within the manufacturer-defined limits; specimens exceeding the limit are rejected.
Centrifugation, plasma separation, and storage follow the manufacturer's instructions; freeze-thaw history is tracked for specimens not tested immediately.
Two levels of control are run each day of testing for quantitative coagulation tests per 42 CFR 493.1256(d); failed QC pauses patient testing until in control.
Patient-specific anticoagulant therapy is considered in the lab's reportable range and interpretation guidance.
procedure_steps: Specimen receipt staff verify tube fill (within manufacturer-defined acceptable range) and time-from-collection.
Acceptable specimens are processed per the procedure (centrifugation per manufacturer's protocol, plasma separated if storage required).
Two control levels are run each day of testing; QC review and corrective action per the Quality Control Plan policy.
definitions: Required 9:1 ratio of blood to sodium citrate anticoagulant in coagulation tubes. Underfill or overfill alters the ratio and invalidates coagulation results.
